vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:deft-directive-setup
vBRIEFInfo.description: Project specification for 3mf QR Code Generator (qr23mf) — Light path

narratives.Overview: qr23mf is a Python CLI that converts an arbitrary string or URL into a 3D-printable mesh of a QR code. It supports single-color STL output and two-color 3MF output with per-body color metadata. Dimensions (base size, base thickness, pixel extrusion height), QR error-correction level, and output format are all configurable via flags, with sensible defaults so the minimum command is `qr23mf --text '<input>' --out <name>`.
narratives.Architecture: Layered, pure-functional Python package. `io` layer wraps `segno` to produce a binary QR matrix from a string input. `geometry` layer converts a matrix plus a parameter struct into two explicit triangle meshes (flat base plate and extruded pixel blocks) using numpy-stl. `writers` layer emits the final artifacts: an STL writer merges the meshes into a single-color binary STL; a 3MF writer assembles a zipped 3MF container with two named bodies and per-body color metadata (via `lib3mf` Python bindings, falling back to direct XML composition if unavailable). `cli` layer is a `typer` front-end that parses flags, validates inputs, and invokes the pipeline. All layers are deterministic given identical inputs (no randomness, no wall-clock dependence) so tests can use golden fixtures.
narratives.Requirements:
FR-1 Accept a string or URL as primary input, with configurable QR error-correction level (L/M/Q/H, default M).
FR-2 Produce a parametric 3D mesh with configurable base size (mm square), base thickness (mm), and pixel extrusion height (mm).
FR-3 Emit outputs in two formats: single-color binary STL (merged mesh) and two-color 3MF (two bodies, body-scope color metadata) selectable via `--format`.
FR-4 Use sensible defaults: size=60 mm, base=2 mm, pixel=1 mm, EC=M, format=3mf.
FR-5 Provide a typer-based CLI (`qr23mf --text ... --out ... [flags]`) with clean `--help` and shell autocompletion support.
NFR-1 Deterministic output: identical inputs produce byte-identical output files across runs and platforms.
NFR-2 ≥85% test coverage overall and per-module, enforced in `task check`.
NFR-3 Install story: `uv tool install .` or `pipx install .` MUST produce a working `qr23mf` console script on PATH.
NFR-4 Runtime dependencies kept minimal: `segno`, `numpy-stl`, `typer`, and (optionally) `lib3mf`.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Pytest-based. Unit tests cover each layer independently (io, geometry, writers, cli). Golden-fixture tests pin the binary output of representative inputs (e.g., a canonical URL at default params) to catch regressions in triangle ordering, face normals, or 3MF XML structure. Coverage gate at ≥85%, enforced via `task test:coverage` as part of `task check`.
narratives.Deployment: Users install via `uv tool install .` or `pipx install .`. No server component; the tool is invoked on-demand from a terminal. Optional follow-up: publish to PyPI as `qr23mf` so `pipx install qr23mf` works without a local clone.
